color.quote: Numerous small dark brown to black lesions are scattered across the green leaflets, with surrounding tissue showing yellow chlorotic patches especially on the upper leaflet.

shape.quote: The lesions appear as tiny pinpoint to small punctate specks, mostly circular and less than a few millimeters in diameter.

margin.quote: The dark specks have somewhat diffuse edges and many are surrounded by faint yellow chlorotic halos rather than sharply defined borders.

texture.quote: The lesions appear flat and embedded in the leaf surface as dry necrotic flecks, with no visible fuzzy growth, ooze, or sporulation.

color.quote: Numerous small dark brown to black specks are scattered across the leaf surface, several surrounded by faint yellowish chlorotic halos against the green leaf tissue.

shape.quote: Most lesions are tiny and roughly circular pinpoint specks, though some have coalesced into larger irregular dark blotches.

margin.quote: Lesion margins appear slightly diffuse with thin pale-yellow chlorotic halos surrounding many of the dark specks.

texture.quote: The dark specks appear dry and necrotic, with some advanced lesions looking slightly sunken and causing leaf curling and tearing.

color.quote: The foliage across the staked tomato rows has turned almost uniformly dark brown to blackish, in stark contrast to the still-red and orange ripening fruit and the surrounding green grass.

texture.quote: The canopy looks dry, shriveled, and largely defoliated — only skeletal brown stems remain on the lower and middle portions of the plants, with fruit hanging exposed on bare stakes.

color.quote: From this wide field view the affected tomato canopy reads as a uniformly dull dark green with no resolvable mosaic mottling or bright chlorotic patches at this distance.

shape.value: stunted plants with narrowed drooping leaflets
shape.quote: Plants between the stakes are noticeably stunted and compact with narrowed, drooping leaflets giving a shoestring-like silhouette compared to the fuller plants further down the row.

margin.quote: Leaflet edges appear curled and rolled downward along the affected plants, but discrete lesion margins cannot be resolved at this field-scale distance.

texture.quote: The foliage on the affected plants looks limp and collapsed over the stake strings with a wilted, drooping texture; fine surface detail such as rugosity, blistering, or sporulation is not visible at this scale.

color.quote: The lesion shows a central white-to-grayish fungal mass surrounded by tan/beige necrotic tissue on an otherwise pale green unripe tomato fruit.

shape.value: roughly circular sunken lesion with radial cracks
shape.quote: A roughly circular sunken lesion sits at the stem-end of the fruit with star-like radial cracks splitting outward through the surrounding tan tissue.

margin.quote: The lesion margin is irregular and jagged where the tan necrotic tissue meets the surrounding pale green fruit surface, with no sharp halo.

texture.quote: The center shows fluffy white mycelial growth within a sunken, collapsed area, while the surrounding necrotic tissue is dry, papery, and cracked.

value: large irregular dark blotches covering much of the fruit surface
cracked tan/cream necrotic patch within one lesion
no visible white sporulation in this photo
distinctive_signs.quote: Three immature green tomato fruits each show large, firm, greasy-leathery dark blotches; the right fruit also has a sunken cracked tan area within the lesion, and no white mold/sporulation is visible.

plant_parts.quote: Only immature green tomato fruits with attached calyx/stem are shown — no leaves or stems are pictured.

color.quote: Lesions are a greasy dark chocolate-brown to olive-grey blending into the pale green unaffected fruit, with a tan/cream cracked spot on the rightmost tomato.

shape.value: Large, irregular, diffuse blotches with poorly defined margins covering 30–60% of each fruit's surface; not concentric or ringed, and one lesion shows a sunken cracked center.
shape.quote: The lesions are broad, irregularly shaped diffuse blotches with feathered edges rather than sharp circular spots, often wrapping over the shoulder of the fruit.

value: brown vascular and pith discoloration revealed by longitudinal stem split
internal browning extends continuously along the stem and into a side branch
outer epidermis remains green while inner tissues are necrotic
distinctive_signs.quote: The longitudinally split stem reveals a continuous brown discoloration of the pith and vascular tissue running the length of the stem and into a lateral branch, while the outer rind stays green.

plant_parts.quote: A tomato stem has been split lengthwise to expose the pith and vascular cylinder, including the junction where a side branch diverges.

value: tan to medium brown internal pith
darker brown streaking along the vascular ring
color.quote: Internal pith and vascular tissue are uniformly tan-brown with darker brown streaks, contrasting sharply with the still-green outer stem walls.

shape.value: Elongated linear streak of brown discoloration running the full length of the split stem and continuing into the branching node, with no discrete spots or lesions on the exterior.
shape.quote: The discoloration takes the form of an unbroken longitudinal brown streak through the central pith rather than discrete external lesions.

color.quote: Three uprooted tomato plants display extensive tan-to-brown discoloration of foliage and stems with only patchy residual green tissue and a few green fruits remaining.

shape.quote: The image shows whole-plant wilt and collapse rather than discrete lesions, with stems flopped and foliage drooping after being pulled from the soil.

texture.quote: Leaves and stems appear dried and shriveled with a brittle, papery look, but no white mycelium or tan sclerotia are resolvable at this distance.

color.quote: The leaflet shows a dull green background overlaid with numerous small bronze to dark purplish-brown flecks, with subtle bronzing concentrated between veins.

shape.value: small irregular specks and scattered flecks
shape.quote: Symptoms appear as tiny irregular specks and flecks scattered across the leaf blade rather than discrete circular lesions.

margin.quote: The flecks have diffuse, poorly defined edges that blend into the surrounding leaf tissue without a sharp halo or border.

texture.quote: The leaf surface looks dull and matte with a faintly puckered, rugose appearance and slight cupping/distortion of the leaflet.
